title: Bilingual Classroom Area Labels, Routines & Rules (K-2)
description: A set of bilingual (English-Spanish) labels for common classroom areas, essential visual routine cards, and classroom rules, designed for Kindergarten to 2nd-grade students, particularly supporting English Language Learners (ELLs).

visual_routine_cards.icon_prompt: restroom door symbol, child washing hands
children sitting in a circle on a rug, teacher reading a book
child waving goodbye, school bus, clock showing end of day

classroom_rules.icon_prompt: child with hand cupped to ear, paying attention
child with hand raised in classroom setting
child walking in a line, footprints

teacher_notes.objective: To create a language-rich and organized classroom environment that supports K-2 ELLs in understanding classroom areas, daily routines, and behavioral expectations through visual and bilingual aids.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Print and laminate all labels, routine cards, and rules for durability. Place full-size area labels prominently on walls or doors of designated zones. Use routine cards on a visible schedule board, reviewing them daily. Display classroom rules in a central location and refer to them regularly during class discussions.
materials_needed: Tape, sticky tack, or velcro dots for display
differentiation_tips: For newcomers, pair labels with realia or gestures when introducing them.
Encourage students to echo the English and Spanish words for each label.
Have students help place labels in the correct areas as a collaborative activity.
Use routine cards to sequence events before they happen, giving students a visual preview.
Incorporate the rules into role-playing scenarios to demonstrate appropriate behavior.
